data.title: As a test-writing agent, I follow a clear testing boundary rule for instruction files so I do not write change-detector tests for non-executable artifacts
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Rule 5 (instruction-file testing boundary) to the shared behavioral testing standard at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/behavioral-testing-standard.md. This rule defines the testing boundary for non-executable artifacts (skills, prompts, agent definitions, protocols): test only at the deterministic integration interface; do NOT test behavioral correctness of LLM instruction content.
**Why**: Without a boundary rule, test-writing agents demand behavioral tests for Markdown artifacts, causing grep-based content checks that are change-detector anti-patterns. This rule eliminates the deadlock.
**Scope**:
- IN: New Rule 5 in behavioral-testing-standard.md defining the instruction-file testing boundary; acceptable structural test categories (contract schema validation, referential integrity, shim compliance, syntax checks, deployment prerequisites); explicit prohibition of test -f existence checks and grep-based content assertions as change-detector tests
- OUT: Changes to implementation-plan SKILL.md (sibling story); changes to TDD plan reviewer (sibling story); changes to agent definition files (agents auto-consume via path reference)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the shared behavioral testing standard contains a Rule 5 that defines the testing boundary for non-executable artifacts: test only at the deterministic integration interface (contract schema validation, referential integrity, shim compliance, syntax checks, deployment prerequisites like test -x); do NOT test behavioral correctness of LLM instruction content
  ← Satisfies: "The shared behavioral testing standard contains a rule defining the testing boundary for non-executable artifacts"
- When this story is complete, the shared behavioral testing standard lists acceptable structural test categories and explicitly prohibits test -f existence checks and grep-based content assertions as change-detector tests
  ← Satisfies: "The shared behavioral testing standard contains the acceptable structural test categories and explicitly prohibits test -f existence checks and grep-based content assertions as change-detector tests"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Structural test categories must not contradict the pre-commit test quality gate's anti-pattern rules -- verify compatibility before finalizing
- [Maintainability] This story is the walking skeleton -- all other stories depend on it being merged first
